messages.text: For the adoption form, I built a reusable MultiStepForm component. The pattern is: zod schema in /lib/schemas/, shared between the form component and the server action. Frontend validates on each step, server action re-validates everything on submit. Don't duplicate validation logic — one schema, two consumers.
nice, that's clean. I'll follow the same pattern for any forms I build
exactly. if we keep this consistent across all forms, onboarding new devs is way easier — they learn one pattern and it works everywhere

heads up — for the donation form I used the same zod schema approach. schema is at /lib/schemas/donation.ts. I went with Stripe Elements over Checkout Sessions because we need custom form styling to match our design system. Tradeoff: more code but full UX control.
makes sense. the donation page looks great in the design, we definitely needed custom styling there

<@U_SARAHKIM> for the contact form — use the same zod schema pattern. Create /lib/schemas/contact.ts with the validation, share it between the ContactForm component and the server action. Same approach as adoption and donation. Don't reinvent the wheel.
got it, I'll follow the pattern. quick question — should the form submission go through an API route or a server action?

always use server actions for form mutations, not API routes. we hit auth token refresh issues with API routes back in March — the token would expire mid-submission and the error handling was messy. server actions handle the session automatically. this applies to all future forms too.
makes sense, server actions it is. thanks
question about the image upload flow — when a staff member uploads a pet photo, does it go through our server or straight to S3?

straight to S3 via presigned URLs. the backend generates a presigned upload URL, the client uploads directly to S3, then calls /api/images/register to save the record. this way our server never handles the large file. CloudFront serves the images with a 24h cache TTL.
why not use Vercel Image Optimization?

our images are user-uploaded and often 5-8MB. Vercel's optimizer has size limits and adds latency on the first request. since we control the upload pipeline, we ensure quality at upload time and let CloudFront handle caching. cleaner separation of concerns.
ok makes sense. I'll use plain img tags with the CloudFront URL for the gallery then
animal browse pages — should those be SSR or ISR?

ISR with 60s revalidate. the animal data doesn't change that frequently and we want the pages to load fast. the donation page is SSR because we need real-time Stripe session data. for anything with live inventory-type data (like available animals count on the homepage), use SSR. for catalog pages, ISR is fine.
got it, ISR for browse, SSR for donation. I'll make sure the event pages are ISR too since they're mostly static
where does the shelter location data live? I need it for the event detail page
shelters table in the database. check the schema doc in Confluence. each shelter has address, city, state, zip, phone, email, and hours as JSONB
we should look into adding Sentry for error monitoring. right now we rely on Vercel's function logs and it's pretty hard to debug production issues
agreed, I've been meaning to set that up. let's add it after we finish the current sprint. it's straightforward with Next.js — just the @sentry/nextjs package and a couple config files
<@U_JAKRIVERA> for the RSVP form on the events page, make sure you use a server action, not an API route. same reason as the other forms — server actions handle auth session properly.
yep already doing that. schema is in /lib/schemas/event-rsvp.ts, following the pattern
perfect. consistency across all forms makes life easier for everyone.

the MultiStepForm component Matt built is at /components/MultiStepForm.tsx. it takes a steps array where each step has a title, a zod schema, and a render function. progress indicator is built in. gonna use it for the RSVP form too
does it handle going back to previous steps?
yeah, you can go back and the data persists. it stores each step's state separately. only the final submit calls the server action
what's our color palette for buttons? I'm seeing inconsistency between the adoption form and the donation page
primary actions should use bg-emerald-600 (our brand green). secondary actions use bg-slate-200. destructive is bg-red-500. it's in the tailwind.config.ts under the custom colors
ok I'll update the design tokens doc to make this explicit. we need Mia's approval on the exact shades
the mobile hamburger menu is breaking when the viewport is exactly 768px — it shows both the mobile and desktop nav. classic Tailwind breakpoint issue
use md:hidden and md:flex instead of lg breakpoints, that should fix it. we should standardize on md as the mobile/desktop cutoff
fixed, thanks. also updated the nav component to match
for the animal profile page, I'm rendering the description as markdown. using react-markdown. is that what we do elsewhere?
yep, the event descriptions also use react-markdown. make sure to sanitize with rehype-sanitize plugin — Matt was strict about that
got an email from a family — they want to donate toys and blankets to the shelter but the website only lets you donate money. apparently they have a bunch of dog toys their pet outgrew
that's a good idea actually. a lot of people probably have old pet supplies sitting around
yeah but where does it go on the site? do we add it to the donation page or make a whole new page? and do we need to store shipping addresses? Matt would need to decide the architecture for this
also we don't have any shipping/address fields in the database. would need a new table or at least new columns
let's talk about it when Matt is back next week. I don't want to make architecture decisions without him
Matt is actually out all next week too — he's at a family thing until the 24th
ok so this just waits then I guess. I'll add it to the backlog and we can discuss when he's around
another feedback — someone submitted an adoption application two weeks ago and never heard back. says there's no way to check the status on the website. this is the third time we've heard this
yeah PAW-15 covers this — Sarah is working on the status tracking page. should be up soon
good. it's a bad look when applicants feel ghosted. let's make PAW-15 a priority
someone suggested we should add a transparency page showing how donations are used — like "your $25 feeds 5 animals for a week." I think that could really boost donation conversion
that's a great idea from a marketing angle. we could pull actual numbers from the donations table and the animals table. like total donated this month, number of animals fed, adopted, etc.
love it. let's create a ticket for that. it would also look great on social
a volunteer mentioned the photo gallery on the animal profile pages is hard to use on mobile — can't swipe, photos are small. she said a lot of potential adopters browse on their phones
that tracks with the customer feedback we got about wanting more/better photos. the gallery was a v1 implementation, probably needs a refresh
welcome to the team @sarah.kim! glad to have you on board. Matt can walk you through the codebase this week
thanks everyone! excited to be here. i'll start getting familiar with the repo today

Sarah — the engineering overview doc in Confluence is a good starting point. also check out the database schema doc. for the codebase itself, start with /app for pages and /lib for shared utilities. the form pattern (zod schemas + server actions) is used everywhere so once you learn that you're good
big milestone — we hit 200 successful adoptions through the platform! 🎉
amazing! we should put something about this on the homepage
good idea, maybe a live counter? we have the data in the adoption_applications table where status = 'approved'
reminder: adoption event this Saturday at the downtown shelter. bring friends!
heads up I'll be out June 10-24 for a family trip. Sarah and Jake have context on everything in progress. anything urgent can wait or ping me on signal if the site is down
enjoy the trip Matt! we'll hold the fort
have fun! we'll try not to break anything while you're gone 😄
